entities.name: pada
entities.cite: pada is equally the grammarian's word for a word

entities.name: siddhi
entities.cite: the first foothold on the ladder of siddhi is also the standing-place of the word

entities.name: mārga
entities.cite: a road that is not crooked is not merely grand but shorter

entities.name: viparyāsa
entities.cite: viparyasa is inversion, taking one thing for another

entities.name: vedajña
entities.cite: the Veda-knower beholds a body made of Veda

entities.name: chandomayī tanū
entities.cite: the sole body made of Veda

axioms.cite: the Veda-knower beholds a body made of Veda which is at the same time the yoni, the womb, of the Vedas
its identification with atman closes the circuit opened in 1.1, where Brahman was declared word-essence

axioms.name: pada_sabda
axioms.cite: pada is equally the grammarian's word for a word

axioms.name: asraya
axioms.cite: the first foothold on the ladder of siddhi is also the standing-place of the word

axioms.name: niyati
axioms.cite: The pair completes the sequence begun at 1.11 by naming first the road and then what stands at the end of it

denials.name: viparyasta
denials.cite: the vision opens only once it has fallen away

denials.name: bhinna
denials.cite: its identification with atman closes the circuit opened in 1.1, where Brahman was declared word-essence

claims.name: asraya
claims.cite: This is the first foothold on the rungs of the ladder of attainment

claims.name: niyati
claims.cite: this is the straight highroad for those seeking liberation
the sole body made of Veda — the womb of the Vedas, the Self

rejected_readings.label: pada_as_property
rejected_readings.rendering: Words are merely linguistic tools for communicating about reality.
rejected_readings.why: Demotes pada to a linguistic property, erasing the double sense (foothold-and-word) that makes it both a means and the metaphysical foundation of realization. The sort error breaks the pun and the claim that grammar is the direct path.

rejected_readings.label: dual_self_and_veda
rejected_readings.rendering: The Veda-knower perceives the body made of Veda as something other than the Self.
rejected_readings.why: Introduces dualism between chandomayi_tanu and atman, contradicting the axiom of their identity. The non-dual recognition is the entire force of the vision.

rejected_readings.label: plural_equal_paths
rejected_readings.rendering: Ritual practice and meditation provide equally direct paths to liberation.
rejected_readings.why: Contradicts the commentary's claim that 'grammar's superiority lies in going directly' — the uniqueness of directness is central, not plurality of equivalent paths.

notes: The verse encodes two core insights: (1) pada's double meaning as both a foothold (means) and a word (metaphysical substance), and (2) the non-dual vision where the manifest body of Veda, the source of Vedas, and the Self are identical. The path (marga) is grammar's directness, and it leads to the realization of this non-duality. Contested: the grammatical structure of 1.17 admits multiple readings; the contract encodes the singular-object reading (chandomayim tanum).
